title: Iterator protocol
body:
## Definition

An **iterable** exposes `Symbol.iterator` returning an **iterator** with a `next()` method yielding `{ value, done }` — powers `for...of` and spread.

## Why it exists

Uniform iteration over arrays, strings, Maps, Sets, and custom collections.

## How it works

```js
const iter = [1, 2][Symbol.iterator]();
iter.next(); // { value: 1, done: false }

function* range(n) {
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) yield i;
}
```

Generators implement the iterator protocol automatically.

## Common confusion

Iterables are not iterators — `for...of` calls `Symbol.iterator` to get a fresh iterator. Plain objects are not iterable by default.

## In practice

Custom data structures, lazy sequences, and async iterators (`for await...of`).
bodyLinked:
## Definition

An **[iterable](concept:iterator)** exposes [Symbol.iterator](concept:iterator) returning an **[iterator](concept:iterator)** with a `next()` method yielding `{ value, done }` — powers [for...of](concept:for-loops) and spread.

## Why it exists

Uniform [iteration](concept:iterator) over arrays, strings, Maps, Sets, and custom collections.

## How it works

```js
const iter = [1, 2][Symbol.iterator]();
iter.next(); // { value: 1, done: false }

function* range(n) {
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) yield i;
}
```

[Generators](concept:generator) implement the [iterator protocol](concept:iterator) automatically.

## Common confusion

[Iterables](concept:iterator) are not [iterators](concept:iterator) — [for...of](concept:for-loops) [calls](concept:call-apply-bind) [Symbol.iterator](concept:iterator) to [get](concept:getters-setters) a fresh [iterator](concept:iterator). Plain objects are not [iterable](concept:iterator) by default.

## In practice

Custom data structures, lazy sequences, and [async](concept:async-await) [iterators](concept:iterator) (`for await...of`).
